initiatives.title: AI Center for Health Innovation and Informatics
initiatives.description: MUSC announced the AI Center for Health Innovation and Informatics as an enterprise hub for responsible AI in health care, research, education, and operations. The center is led by chief AI officer Marylyn Ritchie and includes AI incubation, synthetic data, AI strategy and operations, and education/outreach teams, with initial build-out expected March-June 2026 and full operations in FY27.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates or relies on guardrails for privacy, academic integrity, or responsible use.

initiatives.title: Healthcare Studies AI curriculum and assessment redesign
initiatives.description: MUSC College of Health Professions redesigned the BS in Healthcare Studies program around structured AI integration across curriculum, assessment, and student learning systems. The redesign embeds AI across the curriculum so students practice questioning AI, evaluating limitations, using it ethically, and applying it in health care contexts through applied coursework and a year-long project.
initiatives.significance: This is a clear public example of program-level AI-resilient assessment and curriculum redesign in health professions education.

investment.description: Program redesign effort; no separate public dollar amount disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: The redesign emphasizes ethical use, critical thinking, applied projects, writing, oral communication, and professional judgment rather than replacing student reasoning with AI output.

initiatives.title: MUSC AI Hub mission
initiatives.description: The MUSC AI Hub aims to expand use, understanding, and research of AI across patient care, research, and education.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Healthcare Studies AI support for adult, transfer, and first-generation learners
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: MUSC's HCS redesign explicitly identifies adult learners, community college transfers, first-generation students, and pre-professional students as learner groups needing practical AI preparation connected to health care workforce expectations.

regulatory_context.summary: State and system AI context checked only at high level; institution-level sources carry the profile.

narrative_summary: Medical University of South Carolina shows an AI posture with five documented initiatives across 4 categories. The evidence emphasizes AI Center for Health Innovation and Informatics, MUSC AI Hub mission, and AI/ML/Data Science medical track. Policy maturity is coded as comprehensive based on public guidance, privacy language, governance, or tool controls. The profile also records teaching, research, workforce, equity, or enterprise-tool activity where sources support it. Leadership names, enrollment, GPU counts, and contract values are left null when not found in the cited public sources.

gaps_acknowledged: Chief AI officer Marylyn Ritchie, HCS assessment redesign, and $2.5 million MUSC-UF AI consortium funding were public; enterprise AI platform user counts and contract values were not found.
MUSC references computing infrastructure through the MUSC-UF consortium and AI Center, but no public GPU count or named campus AI supercomputer was found.
HCS subgroup context is public for adult, transfer, first-generation, and pre-professional students, but no quantitative subgroup outcome metrics were found.
No controversy record was added without a reliable institution-specific source.
